commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a host-side daemon socket bind guard, a foreground-start precheck, and race regression coverage to prevent duplicate daemon processes.

Reproducibility: yes. the duplicate foreground-start race has a high-confidence source path and the PR body reports real macOS survivor counts; I did not run the macOS race in this read-only review. The new blocker is also source-reproducible because an accepting socket is treated as live without the existing verified-status checks.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Queue a narrow repair because the PR fixes a real daemon race but the bind guard needs verified liveness before it is safe to merge.

target.title: fix: stop non-client daemon starts from orphaning a running daemon
target.body:
## Summary

Completes the fix for #191. That issue proposed three lock-stepped pieces:
(1) client-side PID reconciliation, (2) a client-side spawn mutex, and (3) a
host-side guard ("on daemon start, if an existing daemon is alive and answering,
exit early; do not start a second daemon").

The merged change for #191 landed (1) and (2), both in `DaemonClient`. They
serialize the path where parallel `mcporter <tool>` invocations each construct a
client. But #191's own headline repro races `mcporter daemon start --foreground`
directly, and that path never touches `DaemonClient`: `daemon start --foreground`
calls `runDaemonHost` straight away. Piece (3) was never landed, so the repro
still reproduces on current `main`.

This PR adds piece (3): a host-side guard at the socket bind, so any start path
(client-triggered, explicit, or launchd) settles to a single daemon.

## What's Included

- `src/daemon/host.ts`
  - Claims the socket under a dedicated `${metadataPath}.bind` lock inside
    `runDaemonHost`.
  - Probes whether the socket is already accepting; if so, exits without
    rebinding instead of unlinking a live socket (the `prepareSocket()` churn
    described in #191).
  - The bind lock is separate from the client's metadata lock so a client
    awaiting readiness while holding that lock cannot deadlock the bind.

- `src/cli/daemon-command.ts`
  - `daemon start --foreground` checks for a running daemon first and exits early,
    so the common case does not spin up a runtime just to bail at the bind.

## Repro and results

Minimal manual repro (any keep-alive config at ./mcporter.json):

    mcporter --config ./mcporter.json daemon stop
    mcporter --config ./mcporter.json daemon start --foreground &
    mcporter --config ./mcporter.json daemon start --foreground &
    sleep 3
    ps -ax | grep -c "[d]aemon start --foreground"   # main: 2, this branch: 1

Reproduced on a second macOS machine against current `main` (with #191's
client-side fix already in). Counts are surviving daemon processes after the race
settles.

| Scenario | `main` (with #191) | This branch |
| --- | --- | --- |
| #191 repro recipe #1 (race 2x `daemon start --foreground`) | 2 | 1 |
| `daemon start --foreground` x5, 5 trials | 2 every trial | 1 every trial |
| detached `daemon start` x5, 3 trials | up to 3 | 1 every trial |
| implicit cold-start (`mcporter list` x10), 3 trials | 1 | 1 |

On `main` each losing start runs `prepareSocket()`, which unlinks the live socket
and rebinds, leaving the previous daemon alive but unreachable (the orphan
accumulation #191 reports). The implicit-cold-start row confirms no regression
and no deadlock from the separate lock.

## Tests

`tests/daemon.integration.test.ts` automates the repro above (races four
`daemon start --foreground` processes, asserts one survivor); it fails on `main`
(multiple survivors) and passes with the guard.

Full suite: 688 passed, 3 skipped (121 files). `pnpm check` clean.

## Relation to #191

#191 prevents redundant launch decisions from `DaemonClient` callers. This
prevents redundant binds from any start path, including the direct `daemon start
--foreground` in #191's own repro, by adding the host-side early-exit #191
proposed. `client.ts` is unchanged.
target.author: zm2231
